name: Self-Preference Bias in Rubric-Based Evaluation of Large Language Models
summary: arXiv:2604.06996v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: LLM-as-a-judge has become the de facto approach for evaluating LLM outputs. However, judges are known to exhibit self-preference bias (SPB)...
description: arXiv:2604.06996v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: LLM-as-a-judge has become the de facto approach for evaluating LLM outputs. However, judges are known to exhibit self-preference bias (SPB): they tend to favor outputs produced by themselves or by models from their own family. This skews evaluations and, thus, hinders model development, especially in settings of recursive self-improvemen...
author: Jos\'e Pombal, Ricardo Rei, Andr\'e F. T. Martins
